phrasal_verbs.meaning: decide not to do sth you agreed to do
produce and start to sell a new product
continue (or help to continue) to the end of sth unpleasant or difficult
do sb's job for them while they are not available
start to employ; accept (work or responsibility)
start doing sth in a determined or enthusiastic way
start working on sth in order to achieve an aim

duty: have a duty to sb/to do

questions.sentence: The **charity / union** at the automotive plant has just voted in favour of an indefinite strike.
It's **company / firm / business** policy for all employees to wear ID badges while on site.
The sharp **raise / rise** in energy costs has hit small manufacturing businesses particularly hard.
Commercial airline pilots are required by law to **retire / resign** at sixty-five.
After decades of service, Mr Okafor now receives a generous **pension / promotion** from his former employer.
We all worked **overtime / promotion** on Sunday to finish the client's order before the deadline.
Have you heard anything back about the **work / job / career** you applied for at that logistics firm?
The advertised **salary / wage** for the position is $48,000 per annum.
Site engineers here receive a daily **pay / wage** calculated on the hours they put in.
She became a permanent member of **employees / staff / employers** at the regional headquarters last spring.

I.title: Word formation (text)

questions.sentence: Although a certain percentage of graduates will still be (1) ...... (employ) twelve months after completing their degree, the majority will have found (2) ...... (employ) well before that.

Many of these will have secured a position through attending a careers expo. Careers expos are held annually at universities across the country. Companies participate to advertise roles, which are often in (3) ...... (manage), along with the salary packages and career progression that go with them.
Careers expos are an (4) ...... (effect) way for final-year students to explore the kinds of opportunities available to them.

If you attend a careers expo, dress (5) ...... (profession). Avoid casual clothing like jeans and trainers. A smart jacket and trousers make a far better impression.
